primary_accounting_issue: Hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend receivable recognized in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: IFRS 9 establishes the hedge accounting framework and specifies that only items with external parties qualify as hedged items, but contains an exception for intragroup monetary items with FX exposure
IAS 21 governs recognition of foreign exchange differences on monetary items, including intragroup dividend receivables
IFRIC 16 specifically addresses hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements and the treatment of intragroup items

supporting_authority.reason: IFRS 10 establishes the consolidated financial statements context where intragroup transactions are eliminated but FX exposures may remain
IAS 7 classifies dividend cash flows as financing activities and requires disclosure of changes in liabilities from financing activities

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses debt extinguishment by equity issuance, not relevant to dividend FX hedging
Addresses non-cash dividend distributions, not applicable to FX hedge documentation
Insurance contracts standard, not relevant to dividend receivable hedging

treatment_families.family: Recognition vs. deferral of FX effects on intragroup monetary items
Hedge accounting type selection for FX exposure on dividend receivable

approaches.label: Direct FX recognition without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IAS 21, FX differences on monetary items are generally recognized in profit or loss; hedge accounting is optional and not required

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of FX risk on dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits designating the FX fair value change of the receivable as a hedged item in a fair value hedge, subject to the intragroup exception in paragraph 6.3.6

approaches.label: Hedge of net investment in foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 confirms that FX risk on intragroup monetary items can be hedged in consolidated financial statements when functional currencies differ, using net investment hedge accounting
